channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Razer Unveils Viper V4 Pro and Gigantus V2 Pro as New Esports Marvels
item.content:encoded:
Razer has officially uncovered the latest iteration in its esports lineup, the Viper V4 Pro. In addition, the Razer Gigantus V2 Pro was revealed, launching alongside the all-singing, all-dancing esports mouse.

The Viper V4 Pro emerges as one of the lightest, fastest, and most precise gaming mice on the market, providing the world’s greatest esports pro players with cutting-edge technology and the latest Razer-based innovations.

Viper V4 Pro is Unbelievably Fast on Paper

In a new press release, Razer sung the praises of the Viper V4 Pro, a white-hot esports mouse with some serious specs on paper.

The Viper V4 Pro delivers latency that can average as low as 0.204 ms for clicks and 0.36 ms for motion, leaning on 8,000 Hz polling in both wireless and wired modes. That’s blistering. The team also revealed that the Viper V4 Pro is registering 2.5 times faster in motion latency and has nearly double the battery life compared to ‘equivalent wireless gaming mice options.’

That double battery life comes in at a whopping 180 hours, by the way.

Razer has confirmed that the Viper V4 Pro boasts the next-generation Focus Pro 50K Optical Sensor Gen-3 under the hood, capable of up to 50,000 DPI, 930 IPS, and 90 G acceleration. It also comes with frame sync, a landmark technology that eliminates even more motion latency, allowing for the most responsive and accurate aim in the business.

The optical switches can withstand 100 million clicks, the scroll wheel is ‘3.3 times more reliable and consistent’, and it weighs as little as 49 grams but remains reinforced and robust.

Paired with the new Razer Gigantus V2 Pro, a five-tier mouse mat system that offers a range of friction and foam options, the Viper V4 Pro is undoubtedly set to be a fine addition to the esports peripherals space.

In a statement, the Global Esports Director at Razer, Jeffrey Chau, alluded to that fact:

In esports, matches swing on margins as thin as a few milliseconds. At that level, players can’t afford guesswork. They rely on what’s proven to win, which is why Razer has become the top choice of pro players.

The Viper V4 Pro is engineered to deliver faster, more consistent inputs where it matters most: live competition. Combined with the Gigantus V2 Pro’s speedspecific surfaces, this is a loadout built not just to keep up with the fastest plays in the game, but to outpace them.

The Razer Viper V4 Pro costs $159.99, and the Gigantus V2 Pro costs $49.99. Both are available now at Razer.com and stores worldwide.

Do you think the Viper V4 Pro is something you’d buy? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that OpTic Texas’s win streak has been stopped

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Two LEC Roadtrips in France And Spain Confirmed For Spring Split
item.content:encoded:
The LEC has confirmed two more Roadtrips for the 2026 Spring Split, hosted by Movistar KOI (MKOI) and Karmine Corp (KCorp).

Roadtrips are a more recent addition to the League of Legends esports league in EMEA as LEC teams independently host arena shows away from the LEC’s home in Berlin. There was talk that the LEC would move online and out of Berlin next year, but Riot has denied these rumours.

This year, Riot Games has expanded the LEC Roadtrips to last for three days, taking place over long weekends during the LEC Spring Split regular season.

Following on from the LEC Versus finals in Barcelona, MKOI is bringing the LEC back to Spain for a series of matches. And, just like last year, KCOrp is hosting games at its own venue in Évry, France.

Karmine Corp LEC Évry Roadtrip Spring 2026

KCorp will host the first LEC Roadtrip of Spring from April 24 to April 26, during the fifth week of the regular season at the  Les Arènes de Grand Paris Sud venue in Évry-Courcouronnes.

KCorp, Fnatic, Shifters, and Natus Vincere will compete across the three-day event with the following schedule:

Friday, April 24:

  • 16:45 (CEST) – SHFT vs FNC
  • 18:30 (CEST) – KC vs NAVI

Saturday, April 25:

  • 16:45 (CEST) – NAVI vs FNC
  • 18:30 (CEST) – KC vs SHFT

Sunday, April 26:

  • 16:45 (CEST) – SHFT vs NAVI
  • 18:30 (CEST) – KC vs FNC

Movistar KOI LEC Madrid Roadtrip Spring 2026

The next LEC Roadtrip of the Spring Split will come during the final week of the regular season as teams lock in their Playoff places. There will then be a two-week break before the LEC Spring Playoffs begin.

MKOI will host the event at the 12,000-seater Madrid Arena from May 8 until May 10 with MKOI, KCorp, G2 Esports, and GIANTX competing across the following schedule:

Friday, May 8:

  • 16:00 (CEST) – KC vs G2
  • 19:00 (CEST) – GX vs MKOI

Saturday, May 9:

  • 16:00 (CEST) – G2 vs GX
  • 19:00 (CEST) – MKOI vs KC

Sunday, May 10:

  • 16:00 (CEST) – KC vs GX
  • 19:00 (CEST) – MKOI vs G2

Would you be happy with the LEC moving online if it meant more Roadtrips and arena shows? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And find out how G2 Esports brought hope back to LEC fans after First Stand.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Pokémon Champions Battle Pass Pricing Revealed for Japan
item.content:encoded:
The Japan pricing details for Battle Passes in Pokémon Champions have been revealed. Check out all the options below.

The Pokémon Company just announced the release date for their highly anticipated game, Pokémon Champions, which will be available very soon on Switch and Switch 2. Now, as we inch closer to the release, the developers are providing additional details on microtransactions and pricing.

Pokémon Champions Battle Pass Costs 1400 Yen a Season

The details on Pokémon Champions Battle Pass pricing come through a reliable source, Serebii.net. The latest update reveals that the game’s battle pass has been priced at 1400 Yen a season (approximately $9), and the Starter Pack, which includes 50 Training Tickets, 30 Teammate Tickets, 50 additional box space, and music from Pokémon Let’s Go, costs 980 Yen (approximately $6). Lastly, the membership costs 700 Yen a month (approximately $4) or 7,000 Yen a year (approximately $44).

The above pricing in USD is just an approximation; the developers have yet to reveal the official prices for the battle pass in the United States and Europe. A previous post states that battle passes will include items, Mega Stones, icons, and more, and players will complete them by grabbing points in season battle scores in Ranked Battle. Developers should provide more details on the battle pass closer to release.

In other news, the first VGC events to use Pokémon Champions have been revealed. Additionally, Pokopia’s recent update has fixed issues with many broken quests. What are your thoughts on the Pokémon Champions Battle Pass pricing in Japan? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Epic Games laying off over 1,000 employees. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Pokémon Reveals First VGC Events Using Pokémon Champions Ahead of Worlds 2026
item.content:encoded:
The Pokémon Company has announced the major events where Pokémon Champions will debut ahead of Worlds 2026 in San Francisco, with major events in May outlining the transition to the new game.

Pokémon Champions was already announced as the platform that the VGC portion of the 2026 World Championships would be competed on last year, and, following the announcement of the release date, two events are confirmed to be using the game ahead of Worlds.

Pokémon Champions Debuts at Indianapolis Pokémon Regional Championships

Pokémon Champions is releasing on Nintendo Switch systems on Wednesday, April 8, and the first official Pokémon Championship Series event to use the new game will be the Indianapolis Pokémon Regional Championships.

That competition is scheduled to run from May 29 to May 31, with competitor registration opening next week, and it’s also been confirmed that the North America International Championships (NAIC) from June 12 to 14 will also have the VGC aspect take place on Pokémon Champions.

Pokémon Champions’ debut is a significant step forward for VGC in the franchise, which has previously taken place on the most recent generation of games.

Pokémon Scarlet & Violet were the most recent games used in VGC, but that comes with significant restrictions, as not every Pokémon is available in the games.

Additionally, the return of Mega Evolution to the Pokémon franchise with the release of Pokémon Legends: Z-A would not have impacted the VGC scene without the launch of Pokémon Champions, as Scarlet & Violet does not support Mega Pokémon, and Legends: Z-A uses a different battle format.

Pokémon Champions will become the mandatory platform for CP events in the 2027 season, starting September 1.

What are your thoughts on the launch of Pokémon Champions? Share your views in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, read about the Mega Shine set coming to Pokémon TCG Pocket, and check out the full list of Shadow Pokémon in XD: Gale of Darkness.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Epic Games Cuts 1,000 Employees In Mass Layoffs
item.content:encoded:
Epic Games is holding mass layoffs that will see more than 1,000 people lose their jobs.

In one of the larger mass layoffs to hit the gaming industry in recent years, Epic Games announced the move in a post on the company’s website on Tuesday morning.

“I’m sorry we’re here again,” Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney said. “The downturn in Fortnite engagement that started in 2025 means we’re spending significantly more than we’re making, and we have to make major cuts to keep the company funded. This layoff, together with over $500 million of identified cost savings in contracting, marketing, and closing some open roles puts us in a more stable place.”

Sweeney said that some of the challenges the company is facing are “industry-wide challenges.” However, some are “unique to Epic” as well.

  • More Layoffs: Red Storm Was Working On At Least 10 Games At Time Of Mass Layoffs

“Despite Fortnite remaining one of the most successful games in the world, we’ve had challenges delivering consistent Fortnite magic with every season,” he said.

He then makes it clear that these layoffs are not related to AI.

Sweeney spoke about the company going through major struggles at other points over the last 30 years. However, he says, what’s happening now is something different.

“Market conditions today are the most extreme we’ve seen since those early days, with massive upheaval in the industry accompanied by massive opportunity for the companies that come out as winners on the other side,” he said. “That’s what we’re aiming to do for our players, and we aim to bring other like-minded developers in the industry along on the journey to build an increasingly open and vibrant future of entertainment together.”

Employees being let go will receive at least four months of salary, with more based on tenure with the company. Epic also says that employees in the US will receive six months of paid health care as well as stock option vesting being accelerated through January 2027.

This isn’t the first time that Epic has held layoffs of this scale. Back in 2023, the company let go of 830 employees.

In other news, read about what Capcom said regarding AI and how it plans to use it moving forward. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/epic-games-1000-employees-mass-layoffs/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokémon Champions Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The Pokémon Company has announced that Pokémon Champions will release on April 8, 2026, for Nintendo Switch and Switch 2.

During the Pokémon Presents showcase on Pokémon Day, a release window for Pokémon Champions on consoles was revealed, pointing to April 2026. A mobile release was also mentioned, but that is scheduled to arrive later this year. The developers have now announced starter pack bundle details and other information regarding the upcoming game.

Pokémon Champions Releases for Switch and Switch 2 on April 8

The latest blog post by The Pokémon Company confirms the Pokémon Champions release date for Switch and Switch 2. The developers have revealed a Starter Pack bundle that provides additional in-game benefits, including increased Pokémon storage space, an extra battle song, and more. Moreover, the Switch 2 version will receive an update that will improve the title’s visuals.

As you might already know, Pokémon Champions enables players to face other players in Pokémon battles with the creatures they have collected across previous games using Pokémon Home. Players will also encounter other Pokémon in the game, including Mega Meganium, Mega Emboar, and Mega Feraligatr.

The three Mega Pokémon feature special abilities, with Mega Meganium receiving Mega Sol that lets the “Pokémon use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight”. Mega Emboar gets Mold Breaker, which makes its moves unaffected by the target’s Ability. Lastly, Mega Feraligatr’s Ability is Dragonize, which turns Normal-type moves into Dragon-type moves and boosts their power by 20%.

In other Pokémon news, here’s the release date for the Mega Shine booster pack for the TCG Pocket. Additionally, Pokopia’s recent update has fixed issues with many broken quests. Will you be playing Pokémon Champions once it releases next month? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about GreedFall dev and three other Nacon studios filing for insolvency. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
